  "decision": "fail",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Aluminum conductors terminated on a device not rated CO/ALR — incompatible termination creating fire hazard",
    "Aluminum-to-non-CO/ALR-device connection will oxidize and loosen over time, causing overheating and potential arc fault"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace all devices receiving aluminum conductors with CO/ALR-rated receptacles/switches",
    "Inspect all device terminations for aluminum conductor use and verify CO/ALR marking on device bodies",
    "Torque all terminations to manufacturer specifications after device replacement"
  ],
  "rationale": "Aluminum conductors connected to devices not rated CO/ALR are a known fire hazard. Aluminum expands and contracts differently than copper, and without a CO/ALR-rated termination designed to accommodate this behavior, the connection loosens, oxidizes, and overheats. This is a direct code violation and a critical safety defect that must be corrected before any close-in or energization.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 310",
    "Table 310.16",
    "NEC 404.14(C) — CO/ALR marking requirement for devices with aluminum conductors",
    "NEC 406.2 — receptacle rating and type for aluminum conductors"
  ]
}
